title: Fix score push guidelines persistence
description: Score push can create a ScoreVersion without the expected guidelines. Behavior needed: pushes must read the canonical local guidelines path, include guidelines when local guidelines exist, and preserve the current champion guidelines on code-only pushes instead of silently omitting them. Verification should cover both CLI push logic and the Score model helper path.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Started investigation. Raw getScoreVersion for champion ef9666a4-dc57-4613-92aa-f74325f347fa currently has non-empty guidelines (12,217 chars), but the push code has two risks: the Score model helper reads root <score>.md instead of the canonical guidelines/<score>.md path, and both push paths can omit guidelines instead of preserving champion guidelines on code-only pushes.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Fixed production data first: rewrote Individual Confirmation champion ScoreVersion ef9666a4-dc57-4613-92aa-f74325f347fa guidelines from the local canonical file and verified 12,217 chars via score.info. Then fixed push behavior: Score model now uses the canonical guidelines/<score>.md path; create_version_from_code preserves champion guidelines when guidelines are omitted and allows explicit empty string clears; CLI push preserves champion guidelines when the local guidelines file is missing and sends guidelines explicitly on create. Verification: /Users/ryan/miniconda3/bin/python -m pytest plexus/dashboard/api/models/score_push_guidelines_test.py plexus/cli/score/score_push_guidelines_test.py -q passed 5 tests; py_compile passed for changed modules and tests.
